title: Spec dependency names can escape project-scoped paths
reasoning: imports and requires are accepted as arbitrary strings, then used directly to build spec paths and generated source paths. A spec can name a dependency such as '../external' or '../specs/taskstore', causing Mint to read a spec outside the intended specs/<module>.mint.md namespace and to compute or pass generated paths outside generated/<module>. Because required modules also feed render ordering and runtime paths, this breaks the project directory boundary and can lead to reads or later writes in unintended locations.
reproduction: Create a spec whose frontmatter contains requires: [../external] with ../external.mint.md present, or requires: [../specs/taskstore] in a project that has specs/taskstore.mint.md. Healthcheck/render dependency resolution will accept the reference string and derive paths from it instead of rejecting it as an invalid module slug.
recommendation: Validate every imports/requires item with the same module slug rules used by new_module, reject path separators, '.', and '..', and have _spec_loader verify that the parsed spec.module exactly equals the requested module name.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included specs tests cover self-require rejection and normal imports/requires parsing, but do not include invalid dependency names or traversal-like module references.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests that parse or healthcheck specs with imports/requires values like '../base', 'base/child', '.', and '..', asserting MintError before any filesystem path is derived outside specs/.
minimumFixScope: src/mint_cli/specs.py validation plus a defensive equality check in src/mint_cli/workflow.py _spec_loader.

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist, but the vulnerable behavior is now guarded. src/mint_cli/specs.py validates imports/requires through MODULE_REF_RE and validate_module_refs at lines 14 and 142-143/273-279, rejecting '../base', 'base/child', '.', and '..'. src/mint_cli/workflow.py still derives spec/generated paths at lines 207-211 and hashes required modules at lines 315-331, but _spec_loader now checks parsed spec.module equals the requested module at lines 292-299. Regression coverage exists in tests/test_specs.py:131-136 and the targeted pytest run passed (10 passed). I also reproduced the reported traversal shape in a temporary project: parse_spec_file rejected '../external', and _spec_loader rejected a direct '../external' load because the parsed module did not match the requested name.
commands: rg -n "def parse_spec_file|requires|imports|class ModuleContext|def compute_context_hashes|resolve_required_order|_spec_loader" src/mint_cli/specs.py src/mint_cli/workflow.py tests

rg -n "MODULE_REF_RE|validate_module_refs|Spec module .*requested|test_dependency_names_must_be_module_slugs|def _spec_loader|def spec_path|def generated_dir_for|def compute_context_hashes" src/mint_cli/specs.py src/mint_cli/workflow.py tests/test_specs.py
